question: Will there be 3 or more total goals in the UEFA Champions League final between Arsenal FC and Paris Saint-Germain?
textDescription:
IMPORTANT INFORMATION: Only goals scored in regulation time (plus stoppage time) will count. Goals scored in extra time will NOT be counted.

The 2026 UEFA Champions League final between Arsenal and Paris Saint-Germain will be played on 30 May 2026 at Puskás Aréna in Budapest, Hungary, with kickoff scheduled for 18:00 CEST.

Resolution criteria

This market resolves to YES if the total number of goals scored by both Arsenal FC and Paris Saint-Germain combined is 3 or more during the match. It resolves to NO if the total number of goals is 0, 1, or 2.

Regulation Time Only: Only goals scored during regulation time (90 minutes plus injury/stoppage time) count toward this total.

Exclusions: Goals scored during extra time or penalty shootouts do not count.

Source of Truth: The official match report published on the UEFA website (https://www.uefa.com/uefachampionsleague/) will be used to determine the final score and goal count. If the match is abandoned before the conclusion of regulation time, the market will resolve based on the official score at the time of abandonment, provided at least 90 minutes have been played; otherwise, it will resolve N/A.

Historical Context
In the last 20 UEFA Champions League finals (2006–2025), 8 of 20 matches (40%) featured 3 or more goals during regulation time. However, recent finals have generally been lower-scoring, with 6 of the last 7 finals (2019–2024) featuring fewer than 3 regulation-time goals.
